opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I went swimming, water got in my ear, and now I'm completely deaf on that side and the pressure is agonizing.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: My right ear suddenly blocked up and became agonizingly painful a few hours ago right after I went swimming, and now I can't hear anything on that side.

symptom_narrative.onset: a few hours ago, right after I went swimming and water got into the ear
symptom_narrative.duration: about 4 hours and it has been getting worse
symptom_narrative.character: a completely full, blocked feeling with intense deep pressure inside the ear
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: agonizing — the worst ear pressure I have ever felt
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it came on after water got in; I tried drying it with a hairdryer and that didn't help
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: a new loud low-pitched roaring noise in that ear, and I feel a bit off-balance and disoriented from suddenly not being able to hear
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: nothing like this before, but I've always used cotton buds to clean my ears
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm very distressed and disoriented by suddenly being deaf on one side

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: My eardrums have always been intact and I've never had any ear surgery.

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: Regularly cleans ears with cotton buds; went swimming today; attempted to dry the ear with a hairdryer afterwards.

objective_data_offered.value: I can't hear my own fingers rubbing right next to my right ear at all, but the left is fine.
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: gross self-administered hearing check reported by the patient, not formal audiometry
